judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable rigor and evidence; Report 2 is slightly more precise on code citations (line numbers) and gives a more actionable fix recommendation for the scheduling image, but the substance is equivalent.

report_1.evidence: **"Export your visit history — Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." ... `app/routers/visits.py` has `# TODO: pdf export` and its `/visits` endpoint returns `[]`. ... **Remove this section (or mark clearly as "coming soon") until it actually ships.** ... **"Stronger account security — Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." ... `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." ... **Must be corrected or removed.**

report_2.evidence: False. `app/routers/visits.py` returns an empty list and carries the comment `# TODO: pdf export` (`visits.py:11`). The feature is unimplemented ... **Remove** this section until the feature ships. ... False, directly contradicted by code: `app/security/config.py:4` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, commented "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic" (`config.py:3`). ... **Remove or correct**

report_1.evidence: The "after" image (`after_scheduling.png`) is **not the scheduling calendar at all** — the image itself renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES," and `captures.json` records its route as `/billing/invoices`.

report_2.evidence: `before_scheduling.png` shows **"SCHEDULING CALENDAR"**; `after_scheduling.png` shows **"BILLING / INVOICES"** — an unrelated screen. `captures.json` confirms it at the source: `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `"/billing/invoices"`, not `/schedule`.

report_1.evidence: **`after_export.png` — referenced (line 26) but missing.** No such file exists; it would render as a broken image. `captures.json` explicitly notes "expected capture; no corresponding file." ... **`unused_capture_07.png` — exists but unreferenced (orphan).** Its content is "PATIENT LIST," unrelated to any section in the doc.

report_2.evidence: **`after_export.png` is referenced but does not exist.** The doc embeds `img/after_export.png` (`highlights-v4.md:26`), but there is no such file in `docs/release/img/`. `captures.json` records it as `"expected capture; no corresponding file"`. ... **`unused_capture_07.png` exists but is referenced nowhere** — it is an orphan image showing "PATIENT LIST," unrelated to any section of the document.

report_1.evidence: **Line 33: "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — an internal drafting/disclaimer note that also flags the piece as an unfinished draft. Remove before sending.

report_2.evidence: **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — This is internal drafting/process metadata (an AI-attribution artifact), not customer content. ... it should not ship in a customer email. **Remove** (or confirm with the owner).
